title: Microchaeta lavarum
taxonomy: Microchaeta lavarum belongs to the family Acanthochortaceae. This species is classified as a type of fern. More specifically, it is a member of the division Pteridophyta. Microchaeta lavarum is native to New Zealand. It is often found in shaded areas of the country's forests.

intro: Microchaeta lavarum, also known as Lavender Microchaeta, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and aromatic foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation due to its calming effects. The bioactive constituents of Microchaeta lavarum include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Microchaeta lavarum is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in aromatherapy blends and skincare products, to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
